title: podman systemd templated containers unable to reference specific instance of templated volume
body:
### Issue Description

subj

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. test-data@.volume [Volume] ServiceName=test-data-volume@ VolumeName=test-%i-data
2. test@.container [Container] ContainerName=test-%i
3. ln -s test@.container test@test3.container
4. test@test3.container.d/override.conf [Container] Mount=type=volume,source=test-data@test1.volume,target=/tmp
5. ln -s test-data@.volume test-data@test2.volume
6. ln -s test@.container test@test4.container
7. test@test4.container.d/override.conf [Container] Mount=type=volume,source=test-data@test2.volume,target=/tmp
8. systemctl daemon-reload
9. systemctl cat test@test3.service
10. systemctl cat test@test4.service
11. systemctl start test@test3.service
12. systemctl start test@test4.service

### Describe the results you received

9 fails to generate

10 gives the following unit

```
...
[Service]
...
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name test-%i ... --mount type=volume,source=test-%i-data,target=/tmp ...
...

[Unit]
...
Requires=test-data-volume@.service
After=test-data-volume@.service
...
```

12 will lead to systemctl start test-data-volume@test4.service due to dependency and instance name equals test4, and test-test4-data volume will be mounted at /tmp

### Describe the results you expected

9 and 10 must both give almost the same result

```
...
[Service]
...
# for 9
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name test-%i ... --mount type=volume,source=test-test1-data,target=/tmp ...
# for 10
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name test-%i ... --mount type=volume,source=test-test2-data,target=/tmp ...
...

[Unit]
...
# for 9
Requires=test-data-volume@test1.service
After=test-data-volume@test1.service
# for 10
Requires=test-data-volume@test2.service
After=test-data-volume@test2.service
...
```

11 and 12 must lead to systemctl start test-data-volume@test1.service and test-data-volume@test2.service respectively due to dependency (instance name should not matter), and test-test1-data and test-test2-data volume must be mounted at /tmp respectively

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 5.6.0 on Fedora 41 x86_64 from distro repos
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details



### Additional information

comments.body:
For workaround one has to do the following in override.conf

```
[Container]
Mount=type=volume,source=test-test1-data,target=/tmp

[Unit]
After=test-data-volume@test1.service
Requires=test-data-volume@test1.service
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm still hitting this on the latest podman.
